description:
## Problem
The HTTP fetch pattern (get pooled client → set headers → GET → follow redirect with SSRF check → check status → return body) is duplicated across:
1. `handle_fetch_and_save` (lines 240-292) — the live synchronous version
2. `do_fetch_google_favicon` (lines 310-363) — Google fallback

After removing the dead `perform_http_fetch` (ticket DEAD1), these two are the remaining duplicates. Both share:
- Pool key construction
- User-Agent header
- Redirect following with Location header parsing and URI resolution
- Status code checking
- Error cleanup (delete from pool, close client)

## Fix
Extract a `fetch_http(url : String, follow_redirects : Bool = true) : {status_code: Int32, content_type: String?, body: Bytes?, error: String?}` helper that handles pooling, redirects, SSRF checks, and error cleanup. Both callers use this helper.

## Depends on
- DEAD1 (remove dead async fetch code) — simplifies the extraction since only 2 paths remain

## File
`src/favicon_storage.cr`
title: DRY: extract shared HTTP fetch-with-redirect helper
